entities.name: JATROPHA
entities.description: Jatropha curcas, a plant used to create biowash

entities.name: ANNONA
entities.description: Annona squamosa, a plant used to create biowash

entities.name: PARTHENIUM
entities.description: Parthenium hysterophorus, a plant used to create biowash

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: A fungal pathogen causing collar rot in chickpea

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. CICERI
entities.description: A fungal pathogen causing wilt in chickpea, abbreviated as FOC

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen causing charcoal rot in sorghum

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A crop susceptible to Sclerotium rolfsii and Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ciceri

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop susceptible to Macrophomina phaseolina

entities.name: BIOWASH
entities.description: Washings from vermicompost of plant foliage used to control plant pathogens

entities.name: VERMICOMPOST
entities.description: Decomposed organic matter used to prepare biowash

entities.name: ETHYL ACETATE
entities.description: A solvent used for partitioning biowash

entities.name: C18 SOLID PHASE EXTRACTION
entities.description: A method used to fractionate biowash

entities.name: METHANOL
entities.description: A solvent used in chromatography, abbreviated as MeOH

entities.name: SILICA GEL 60 F254 TLC
entities.description: A thin layer chromatography method used to separate compounds

relationships.weight: Biowash is derived from Jatropha foliage
Biowash is derived from Annona foliage
Biowash is derived from Parthenium foliage
Biowash inhibits Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ciceri
Sclerotium rolfsii is a pathogen of chickpea
Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ciceri is a pathogen of chickpea
Macrophomina phaseolina is a pathogen of sorghum
Biowash is partitioned using ethyl acetate
Biowash is fractionated using C18 solid phase extraction
Parthenium biowash inhibits Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ciceri
Jatropha biowash is purified to obtain secondary metabolites
Jatropha biowash is fractionated using methanol
Methanol fraction is separated using Silica Gel 60 F254 TLC
Bands on TLC inhibit Sclerotium rolfsii
